proposed_hypotheses.text: At least one binary feature among the 80 binary patient features in the cohort is associated with the probability of objective_response (chi-square test on the 2x2 table; significant if p<0.05).
Among continuous features, mean values differ between objective_response=1 and objective_response=0 for at least one feature (Mann-Whitney U test; significant if p<0.05).

analyses.code: for c in binary_feats: chi2, p, _, _ = stats.chi2_contingency(pd.crosstab(df[c], df['objective_response']))
analyses.result_summary: Across 80 binary features, 4 reached p<0.05 in unadjusted chi-square tests against objective_response. Strongest: feature_035 (response 22.5% vs 16.9%, risk diff +5.6pp, chi-square p=3.5e-18); feature_093 (+1.7pp, p=5.6e-3); feature_121 (-1.4pp, p=0.025); feature_014 (+0.7pp, p=0.031).

analyses.code: for c in cont_feats: u, p = stats.mannwhitneyu(df.loc[y==1,c], df.loc[y==0,c])
analyses.result_summary: Across 37 continuous features, 5 reached p<0.001 vs objective_response. Strongest: feature_011 (mean 3.38 in responders vs 3.95 in non-responders, p=3.4e-30); feature_006 (58.2 vs 60.3, p=1.6e-20); feature_092 (19.1 vs 20.4, p=2.3e-9); feature_099 (3.82 vs 3.80, slightly higher in responders, p=1.3e-6); feature_063 (5.65 vs 6.17, p=3.3e-5).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the multi-level categorical features (feature_057, feature_096, feature_064, feature_045, feature_042, feature_125), feature_057 (3 ordered levels: 0,1,2) shows a monotonic decline in the proportion of patients with objective_response=1 as the level increases.
Other multi-level categorical features (feature_096, feature_064, feature_045, feature_042, feature_125) are also associated with objective_response (chi-square p<0.05).

analyses.code: pd.crosstab(df['feature_057'], df['objective_response']); stats.chi2_contingency(...)
analyses.result_summary: feature_057 shows a strong monotonic decline: response rate 21.3% at level 0 (n=17,592), 15.5% at level 1 (n=24,971), 11.2% at level 2 (n=7,437). Chi-square p=5.5e-98. Adjusted OR (level 1 vs 0)=0.682, OR (level 2 vs 0)=0.465.

analyses.code: for c in [feature_096, feature_064, feature_045, feature_042, feature_125]: chi2, p, _, _ = stats.chi2_contingency(...)
analyses.result_summary: None of feature_096 (p=0.19), feature_064 (p=0.49), feature_045 (p=0.10), feature_042 (p=0.41), feature_125 (p=0.07) reached statistical significance against objective_response. These additional 5-level features are not informative predictors.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with feature_035=1 have a higher probability of objective_response than patients with feature_035=0, after adjusting for the strongest covariates (feature_057, feature_011, feature_006).

analyses.code: logit('objective_response ~ C(feature_057) + feature_035 + f011_z + f006_z', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: feature_035=1 retained a strong adjusted effect: OR=1.43 (95% CI 1.32-1.56), beta=0.358, Wald p=1.85e-17. Effect remains essentially unchanged in models that further include feature_092, feature_099, feature_063, feature_093, feature_121, race, and insurance.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher feature_011 is associated with lower probability of objective_response after adjusting for feature_057, feature_006, feature_035.
Higher feature_006 is associated with lower probability of objective_response after adjusting for feature_057, feature_011, feature_035.

analyses.code: logit('objective_response ~ C(feature_057) + feature_035 + f011_z + f006_z + f092_z + f099_z + f063_z', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: feature_011 is independently inversely associated with response (per +1 SD: OR=0.856, 95% CI 0.835-0.878, p=1.4e-34). Direction confirmed: higher feature_011 -> lower objective_response.

analyses.code: same multivariable model as above
analyses.result_summary: feature_006 is independently inversely associated with response (per +1 SD: OR=0.896, 95% CI 0.875-0.917, p=7.0e-20).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher feature_092 is associated with lower probability of objective_response after adjustment.
Higher feature_099 is associated with HIGHER probability of objective_response after adjustment (the only continuous feature where responders had a higher mean).
Higher feature_063 is associated with lower probability of objective_response after adjustment.

analyses.code: logit('objective_response ~ C(f057) + f035 + f011_z + f006_z + f092_z + f099_z + f063_z', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: feature_092 inversely associated with response: per +1 SD, OR=0.944 (95% CI 0.920-0.968), p=1.1e-5.

analyses.code: same model
analyses.result_summary: feature_099 positively associated with response: per +1 SD, OR=1.061 (95% CI 1.036-1.086), p=9.3e-7. Modest but reliable; only continuous feature with positive direction among top predictors.

analyses.code: same model
analyses.result_summary: feature_063 inversely associated with response: per +1 SD, OR=0.935 (95% CI 0.911-0.961), p=1.3e-6.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patient age (feature_078) is associated with the probability of objective_response.
There is a non-linear (quadratic) relationship between age (feature_078) and objective_response after adjustment.

analyses.code: stratify by qcut(feature_078, 3) and compute response rate; plus add f078_z to logit.
analyses.result_summary: Response rates across age tertiles are essentially flat: young (18-63 yrs) 17.0%, middle (63-73) 16.7%, old (73-92) 17.1%. Adjusted f078 coefficient = 0.003 (p=0.77). Age has no detectable effect on response in this cohort.

analyses.code: logit('objective_response ~ f078_z + I(f078_z**2) + ...', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Quadratic age term not significant (p=0.34) and linear term remains null (p=0.80). No evidence of a nonlinear age effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Race (feature_005) is associated with objective_response, with white patients having a higher response rate than non-white patients.
After adjustment for the strongest clinical covariates (feature_057, feature_035, feature_011, feature_006), race no longer has a statistically significant association with response.

analyses.code: stats.chi2_contingency(pd.crosstab(df['feature_005'], df['objective_response']))
analyses.result_summary: Unadjusted chi-square across race categories p=0.0096. Response rates: white 17.3% (n=32498), hispanic 16.7% (n=7631), asian 16.3% (n=2970), black 15.7% (n=5889), other 14.7% (n=1012). White-vs-other difference is roughly 1-2 percentage points.

analyses.code: logit('objective_response ~ C(f057) + f035 + f011_z + f006_z + C(feature_005)', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: After clinical adjustment, race coefficients are not significant individually (white vs asian reference: beta=0.078, p=0.13; black p=0.64; hispanic p=0.49; other p=0.26). The unadjusted race difference is largely confounded by other measured features.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Insurance type (feature_087) is associated with objective_response.

analyses.code: stats.chi2_contingency(pd.crosstab(df['feature_087'], df['objective_response']))
analyses.result_summary: Response rates across insurance categories are nearly identical: medicare 17.2% (n=20953), medicaid 16.9% (n=6911), private 16.7% (n=20090), uninsured 16.6% (n=2046). Chi-square p=0.54. No association.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The treatment-effect-like association of feature_035 with objective_response is heterogeneous across feature_057 levels (interaction term significant in adjusted logistic regression).

analyses.code: logit('objective_response ~ C(f057)*f035 + f011_z + f006_z', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Stratified rates show f035=1 vs f035=0 risk differences of +6.1pp (f057=0), +5.3pp (f057=1), +4.4pp (f057=2). The two interaction coefficients (f057=1:f035 and f057=2:f035) are not significant (p=0.64 and p=0.67). The main effect of f035 (beta=0.331, p=4.0e-7 within f057=0 reference) is robust; effect modification by f057 is at most modest and statistically non-significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The association of feature_035 with objective_response is modified by feature_011 (interaction term significant).
The association of feature_035 with objective_response is modified by feature_006 (interaction term significant).

analyses.code: logit('objective_response ~ f035*f011_z + C(f057) + f006_z', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: f035 x f011_z interaction beta=-0.053, p=0.24. No significant interaction; f035 effect appears constant across the range of feature_011.

analyses.code: logit('objective_response ~ f035*f006_z + C(f057) + f011_z', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: f035 x f006_z interaction beta=0.041, p=0.32. No significant interaction.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The association of feature_011 with objective_response is modified by feature_057 (per-SD effect of feature_011 differs across the three feature_057 levels).

analyses.code: logit('objective_response ~ C(f057)*f011_z + f035 + f006_z', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Interaction coefficients C(f057)[T.1]:f011_z=0.016 (p=0.56) and C(f057)[T.2]:f011_z=-0.045 (p=0.31) are not significant. The inverse association of feature_011 with response is consistent across feature_057 levels.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The treatment-like effect of feature_035 on objective_response differs between white and non-white patients (race x feature_035 interaction).

analyses.code: logit('objective_response ~ f035*white + C(f057) + f011_z + f006_z', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Interaction f035 x white beta=0.135, p=0.13. Stratified risk differences: white +6.5pp; hispanic +4.1pp; asian +4.0pp; black +4.1pp; other +5.4pp. Direction suggests possibly larger effect in white patients but not statistically significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The treatment-like effect of feature_035 on objective_response differs between Medicare and non-Medicare patients (insurance x feature_035 interaction).

analyses.code: logit('objective_response ~ f035*medicare + C(f057) + f011_z + f006_z', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Interaction f035 x medicare beta=0.165, p=0.053 (borderline). Stratified risk differences: medicare +7.4pp, medicaid +7.2pp, uninsured +5.8pp, private +3.5pp. The smaller f035 effect in privately insured patients explains the borderline interaction; needs cautious interpretation.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_007 (a balanced binary feature) modifies the effect of feature_035 on objective_response.

analyses.code: logit('objective_response ~ f035*feature_007 + C(f057) + f011_z + f006_z', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Interaction f035 x feature_007 beta=-0.061, p=0.47. No effect modification. f035 risk differences: +6.0pp at feature_007=0, +5.1pp at feature_007=1.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_093 (a binary feature with weak unadjusted association) is independently associated with objective_response after adjustment for the strongest predictors.
feature_121 (binary, unadjusted negative association p=0.025) is independently associated with objective_response after adjustment.

analyses.code: Same large multivariable logit including f093 and f121.
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted feature_093 beta=0.126, p=0.003. Confirms a small but real positive association: patients with feature_093=1 have ~2 percentage points higher response after adjustment.

analyses.code: Same model.
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted feature_121 beta=-0.088, p=0.050 (borderline). Suggests a small inverse effect on response (about -1.4 percentage points unadjusted).

proposed_hypotheses.text: When all 9 strongest covariates (feature_057 ordinal, feature_035, feature_093, feature_121, feature_011, feature_006, feature_092, feature_099, feature_063) and race are entered jointly into a logistic regression, every variable except race and feature_121 retains a statistically significant independent contribution.

analyses.code: logit('objective_response ~ C(f057) + f035 + f093 + f121 + f011_z + f006_z + f092_z + f099_z + f063_z + C(feature_005)', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: All clinical predictors retained independent significance: feature_057 [T.1] p=2e-50, [T.2] p=2e-76; feature_035 p=2e-17; feature_093 p=0.003; feature_011 p<1e-30; feature_006 p<1e-19; feature_092 p=1e-5; feature_099 p=1e-6; feature_063 p=1e-6. feature_121 borderline (p=0.050). Race coefficients all p>0.12. Hypothesis supported with a single borderline (feature_121).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among patients in the worst feature_057 stratum (level 2), feature_035 still produces a positive risk difference in objective_response.

analyses.code: df.loc[df['feature_057']==2].groupby('feature_035')['objective_response'].mean()
analyses.result_summary: Within feature_057=2 (n=7,437), response rate is 15.3% with feature_035=1 (n=516) vs 10.9% with feature_035=0 (n=6,921). Risk difference +4.4 percentage points; chi-square p=0.001 within stratum. Effect remains positive in the worst-prognosis stratum.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within feature_057 level 0 (best stratum), feature_035 still produces a meaningful response benefit.

analyses.code: df.loc[df['feature_057']==0].groupby('feature_035')['objective_response'].mean()
analyses.result_summary: Within feature_057=0 (n=17,592), response is 27.0% with feature_035=1 (n=1,331) vs 20.8% with feature_035=0 (n=16,261). Risk difference +6.1 pp; the largest absolute benefit of f035 is in the best-prognosis stratum, but the relative effect (OR) is similar across strata.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_011 (with values 0 to ~24) and feature_057 are themselves correlated (e.g., higher feature_057 levels have higher mean feature_011), explaining part of why both inversely predict response.

analyses.code: df.groupby('feature_057')['feature_011'].agg(['mean','std','count'])
analyses.result_summary: Mean feature_011 by feature_057: level 0 = 3.80, level 1 = 3.90, level 2 = 3.84. Differences are very small (<0.1 SD) and not monotonic. The two predictors are essentially independent and contribute non-redundant information about response.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The remaining ~75 binary features (those not significant in the univariate scan) collectively contribute little additional discrimination over the top 9 predictors.

analyses.code: Compare model log-likelihoods: top-9 model vs top-9 + 'feature_014','feature_030','feature_111','feature_091' (next-best binary features).
analyses.result_summary: Adding the next 4 marginally-univariate binary features changes pseudo-R^2 from 0.0183 to 0.0188 (LLR p borderline). The bulk of the explainable variance is captured by the top 9 features identified in iterations 1-5. Additional binary features contribute very little.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Ordinal trend across feature_057 levels (0 -> 1 -> 2) is statistically significant for objective_response (Cochran-Armitage-style trend in logistic regression with feature_057 as a numeric predictor).

analyses.code: logit('objective_response ~ feature_057 + f035 + f011_z + f006_z', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Treating feature_057 as a numeric trend variable yields beta=-0.398 per level (p<1e-90). Coupled with the consistent monotonic decline (21.3% -> 15.5% -> 11.2%), the ordinal interpretation is strongly supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_006 effect on response is non-linear (quadratic term significant) after adjustment.

analyses.code: logit('objective_response ~ f006_z + I(f006_z**2) + C(f057) + f035 + f011_z', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Quadratic f006 coefficient is small (~ -0.01) with p=0.39, while the linear term remains highly significant (p<1e-19). No meaningful nonlinearity detected for feature_006.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_011 effect on response is non-linear (quadratic term significant) after adjustment.

analyses.code: logit('objective_response ~ f011_z + I(f011_z**2) + C(f057) + f035 + f006_z', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Quadratic f011_z term beta ~ +0.02, p ~ 0.20; linear term retains beta=-0.16, p<1e-30. No strong evidence for a quadratic shape; the inverse relationship is approximately linear on the logit scale.

proposed_hypotheses.text: An interaction between feature_011 and feature_006 (the two strongest continuous predictors) is significant in the adjusted model.

analyses.code: logit('objective_response ~ f011_z*f006_z + C(f057) + f035', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Interaction term f011_z:f006_z beta = -0.005, p ~ 0.7. No evidence that the inverse effects of feature_011 and feature_006 on response interact.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A final 9-predictor logistic regression including feature_057 (ordinal), feature_035, feature_093, feature_121, feature_011, feature_006, feature_092, feature_099, feature_063, plus race produces overall discrimination superior to chance and identifies a stable core set of independent predictors of objective_response.

analyses.code: Final logit model summary; LLR test vs null model.
analyses.result_summary: Final model: pseudo-R^2 = 0.018; LLR chi-square ~ 830, p<1e-170. Independent predictors retained (signed beta on logit scale): feature_057 ordinal -0.40 per step (p<1e-90); feature_035 +0.36 (p=2e-17); feature_011 -0.16 per SD (p<1e-30); feature_006 -0.11 per SD (p<1e-19); feature_092 -0.058 per SD (p=1e-5); feature_063 -0.067 per SD (p=1e-6); feature_099 +0.059 per SD (p=1e-6); feature_093 +0.13 (p=0.003); feature_121 -0.088 (p=0.05); race not independently significant. No statistically robust treatment-effect-modifying interactions were detected (all p>0.05).
